title: Project Metadata, Python Packaging, Entry Points, Plugins, and Clean Installs · Language Clinic: Clean Install
summary: The generator runs only from its repository, imports templates through the current directory, has unbounded dependencies, discovers arbitrary plugins, omits package data, and is never installed into a clean environment. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the clean install increment in installed reproducible static-site command. Learners progress t…
objectives: Build sdist and wheel, inspect contents and metadata, install into fresh supported environments, run the command elsewhere, and verify uninstall boundaries.
Define versioned capabilities, explicit enablement, discovery scope, configuration schema, isolation limits, deterministic order, and failure policy for plugins.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-clean-install — Build sdist and wheel, inspect contents and metadata, install into fresh supported environments, run the command elsewhere, and verify uninstall boundaries.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-packaging-cli-3
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-packaging-cli-3.
# Case 5798: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def worked_static_site_packaging_cli_3(artifacts: int = 2, inspected: int = 2, clean_installs: int = 3, command_smokes: int = 3, unsafe_plugins: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "readerworked3",
        "source5798",
        "parse3dekrow",
        "routeworked35798",
        "artifact5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if artifacts != 2 or inspected < 0:
        return False, inspected, "artifact-shape"
    if inspected != artifacts:
        return False, inspected, "artifact-inspection"
    if clean_installs < 2 or command_smokes != clean_installs:
        return False, command_smokes, "clean-install"
    if unsafe_plugins > 0:
        return False, unsafe_plugins, "plugin-boundary"
    assert inspected == artifacts
    return True, command_smokes, "installed-generator"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Build Clean Install evidence · Theory Clean Install
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-clean-install-2marzb" that uses case 7011 to verify inspected package artifacts, clean command installs, resources, and plugin limits. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Clean Install into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Build Clean Install evidence · Theory Clean Install, keep Clean Install connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-clean-install-2marzb
# Competency: ssg-clean-install.
# Case 7011: a compromised-content recovery rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical.
def evidence_ssg_clean_install_2marzb(artifacts: int = 2, inspected: int = 2, clean_installs: int = 3, command_smokes: int = 3, unsafe_plugins: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_2marzb = "2marzb-bzram2-7011"
    assert evidence_variant_2marzb
    evidence_axes_2marzb = (
        "reader2marzb",
        "source7011",
        "parsebzram2",
        "route2marzb7011",
        "artifact70112marzb",
        "recoverybzram27011",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_2marzb)) == 6
    if artifacts != 2 or inspected < 0:
        return False, inspected, "artifact-shape"
    if inspected != artifacts:
        return False, inspected, "artifact-inspection"
    if clean_installs < 2 or command_smokes != clean_installs:
        return False, command_smokes, "clean-install"
    if unsafe_plugins > 0:
        return False, unsafe_plugins, "plugin-boundary"
    assert inspected == artifacts
    return True, command_smokes, "installed-generator"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Clean Install: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 2373 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to verify inspected package artifacts, clean command installs, resources, and plugin limits; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Build sdist and wheel, inspect contents and metadata, install into fresh supported environments, run the command elsewhere, and verify uninstall boundaries.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Clean Install model · Theory Clean Install.
steps.why: Retrieving Clean Install through the concrete installed reproducible static-site command case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Clean Install model · Theory Clean Install, keep Clean Install connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Build sdist and wheel, inspect contents and metadata, install into fresh supported environments, run the command elsewhere, and verify uninstall boundaries.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Clean Install model · Theory Clean Install, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Clean Install precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Clean Install model · Theory Clean Install, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 2373 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to verify inspected package artifacts, clean command installs, resources, and plugin limits; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Build sdist and wheel, inspect contents and metadata, install into fresh supported environments, run the command elsewhere, and verify uninstall boundaries.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Clean Install model · Theory Clean Install, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: A successful editable install proves the release artifact works.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-clean-install-2marzb.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-clean-install-2marzb(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_clean_install_2marzb\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?artifacts != 2 or inspected < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?inspected != artifacts)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?clean_installs < 2 or command_smokes != clean_installs)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?unsafe_plugins > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using build and sdist plus specific case evidence.
